description: Slice S1 (FGAP-029 P0 safety): /context install --update must NEVER overwrite a populated block. In installContext's blocks loop (packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:396-416), when destExists && overwrite, read the existing block via readBlockForDir and test for items via forEachBlockArray; if any array is non-empty, push to a new InstallResult.preserved[] and skip the copyFileSync. Empty/absent blocks behave as today; the schemas loop is UNCHANGED (schemas still honor --update). Render preserved in the install handler (index.ts:910-942). Add a regression test (none exists today). Per DEC-0010: also add a CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry + usage-only docs (pkg README, monorepo README, SKILL via skill-narrative.md). Reuse readBlockForDir + forEachBlockArray (block-api.ts); installContext is the sole block-writer.
acceptance_criteria: --update against a populated block: block file byte-identical + reported preserved (regression test + runtime demo on a .context backup/dup per the safety protocol, never live .context)
empty existing block still overwritten; a schema under --update still updated (no regression)
no other path overwrites a populated block (fresh adversarial audit; populated-detection correct for object-shape AND flat-array blocks)
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry added; usage-only docs updated (README pkg + monorepo + SKILL) with NO fix-reference
canonical pipeline green: build/check/test + runtime demo + fresh adversarial audit
notes: Slice S1/4 of DEC-0010 (FGAP-029 safe re-sync) | the P0 data-loss closure | NO release at slice boundary — single release after S1-S4
